A

I accidentally started this game instead of Sanctuary so I think oh well, might as well finish it. Future me: (╬≖_≖)
I'm not angry at the game, I'm disappointed, this game is like Dark Souls 2 SotFS, not bad, quite playable but it's riddled with issues. It was kinda weird at first, dark souls 1 combat with MonHun gameplay loop and dark souls 3 / Elden Ring endgame enemy aggression doesn't mix well, still don't at the end, it had some charms with the combat but that's about it, there's nothing else to "praise". 
Nobody has ever said "Oh wow I love chasing monsters in MonHun every 5s instead of just fighting them", that's not why people play MonHun. Chasing the mages around the map every 5s is annoying AF especially when there's no map, there's no reason for the mages to change locations 5 times during one hunt, there's no reason for them to recover so much HP before they finally stop and fight, it's straight up bad design for trying to copy what MonHun did without understanding why they did that.
Enemies seemingly don't stop attacking, the entire combat system seem to based on you spamming light attacks hoping for them to be staggered. Dodge put you so far away from the enemy you can't hit them after a dodge, you can't even control the dodge distance. Bosses do 3-5 hits combos like it's DS3/ER while you stuck with the DS1 slow dodge so you'll be lucky to even get 2 hits before having to start dodging again. There are guard and perfect guard but you get punish so much for missing perfect guard so idk why would anyone even t ry to but I only used Highblade so what do I know. Late game enemies does absurd amount damage and they will chase you to the end of earth, ignoring them is pretty much impossible and fighting them is just so annoying at that point of the game.
In term of enemy placements, it was fine at first but toward later map and especially the finally map, it went full DS2 SotFS, you never go full. I died because of enemies camping on ledges more than I died because of bosses, most bosses don't even take more than a few tries to kill because it's literally just a dps race, I didn't learn a single boss pattern. 
You have to collect resources to craft health potions and range weapon ammos like it's MonHun, why? The game is still mostly a souls-like, it doesn't fit in at all.
There's no map and for a metroidvania that's already a big thumbs down.
Just ignore this game if you want Sanctuary 2 or hate DS2 SotFS.
gostei mt do jogo fiquei com o pé atras no começo mas o jogo e bem divertido igual o primeiro de uma chance a salt and sacrifice

Not bad

Doesn't have the same feel and awe that salt and sanctuary had but it was still fun to just back into a Ska Studio souls-like. I liked Sanctuary way more than this because sanctuary felt more like a souls-like quest throughout the world, where as this game felt like a monster hunter kill then go home kinda thing, didn't feel as immersed and emotionally invested in the world and the things happening in it, another example is i can list 10 bosses from sanctuary, but i can't name 1 boss from Sacrifice. Gameplay wise though it's pretty fun, the combat and exploration itself has been changed a little and it's pretty cool, fighting is pretty much the same but the way you travel around the world is slightly different, different gadgets and ways you move around that are interesting. 

If you liked sanctuary give it a shot, but don't expect the same experience. But me personally i had fun, 6/10
Epic Garbage.
纯纯的一坨
游戏整体比较简单，力量武器冲刺劈削韧削架势无敌，但跳跳乐被法师堵平台边缘很恶心。法师是神怒泄露感染凡人，那其他boss为什么要阻止我们，毕竟我们是去解决问题的。

Не брал на релизе видя тонну негативных отзывов. Спустя какое-то время вышел патч 2.0, который, по словам многих, сделал игру в разы лучше. Если ЭТО в разы лучше, то я не знаю что же было на релизе. Я не буду рассказывать подробности того что в игре сделано плохо или неправильно, лишь поделюсь тем, что вызвало у меня боли в пятой точке за почти 8 часов игры. 

Отсутствие описания у всех стартовых классов. Так как здесь дерево навыков очень сильно напоминает аналогичное в Path of Exile, то стартовые скиллы определяют твое, извините за тавтологию, стартовое положение на карте навыков, то есть всю дальнейшую прокачку. И чтобы найти подходящий класс, мне пришлось пересоздавать персонажа 4 раза просто потому что я не знал кто и в каком месте в древе навыков стартует.

Как продолжение пункта выше - отсутствие какого-либо описания стартовых предметов. Я не фанат лезть на вики как минимум в первом прохождении, но тут пришлось нарушить свое правило дабы понять что вообще я кладу себе в карман на старте. Потому что из описания в игре - картинка предмета. Все.

Обустройство хаба. Ты всегда спавнишься в центре. Статуя для лвлапа в одной конце хаба, до которого бежать секунд 30. Апгрейд и крафт шмота в другом. Телепорт вообще в третьем конце (у нас тут вертикальность, поэтому краев карты как минимум 4) и нет, прилетая в хаб ты спавнишься не около него. Торговцы раскиданы очень хаотично, поэтому найти нового, которого ты привел в последней вылазке, бывает очень сложно. 

Нет карты. Кто-то скажет, что я просто нубас с топографическим кретинизмом. Я скажу да и что метроидвания без карты в 2023 году это какой-то нонсенс. Кто-то на геймспоте писал, что сложности с навигацией только по началу, дальше проще. Дальше не проще. Каждый мир в игре - это огромная разветвленная локация, с кучей дверей и шорткатов, где ты постоянно упираешься в разные стоп сигналы. Вот тут тебе нужен какой-то новый девайс, вот тут надо убить N боссов и так далее. Если поначалу ты еще можешь это держать в голове, то чем дольше ты играешь, тем разветвленнее становится карта. А потом оказывается, что этих миров с полдюжины и каждый из них представляет собой разветвленный и запутанный лабиринт. To make things worse по игре надо скакать из одного мира в другой так как у тебя появляются новые девайсы, позволяющие получить другие новые девайсы в ранее посещенных мирах, чтобы использовать их дабы продвинуться во всех других мирах. Вы поняли, да? Без карты ориентироваться становится невозможно. 

Мне нравится идея скрещивания соулс-лайка с монстер хантером. Что мне не нравится, так что постоянные стычки с магами (местное зверье на которое ты охотишься в поисках ресурсов для крафта). Уже спустя несколько боев ты тыкаешь его своей саблей пару раз чтобы он свалил на другое место и ничего кроме раздражения эти встречи не вызывают. Отдельную боль доставляет мапдизайн, который иной раз дает какое-то невероятное преимущество магам. То он встанет на балкончик меньше его самого вширь и начнет спамить одну единственную атаку, которая не даст тебе к нему подойти потому что во время анимации карабканья он будет бить тебя палкой по лицу, сбивая тебя вниз. То он начнет спавнить гейзеры на земле, которые закрываются фоновым изображением на переднем плане и понять ГДЕ он поставил гейзер дабы избежать урона становится невозможно. То он призывает моба, который делает его неуязвимым, а если этого моба убить, то спустя 3-5 секунд он призывает его заново. Максимально душно. 

Было еще множество мелких моментов, которые просто раздражали. У игры есть потанцевал. Глубокие и продуманные механики, интересные локации, которые так и хочется исследовать. Великолепный стиль и арт дизайн. Одни из лучших и приятнейших механик перемещения в жанре. Но все плюсы игры перечеркиваются жирной линией под названием ублюдочный геймдизайн. Игра и так далеко не короткая, но разработчики будто намеренно создают духоты дабы растянуть геймплей. 

Итог - лучше купите оригинальную Salt and Sanctuary. Она куда лучше своего продолжения.

I went into this thinking the game will be somewhat disappointing based on the reception from other players. What I found instead was one of the most addictive metroidvania/action platformers I have played.

Hunting mages with specific equipment in mind and finetuning the skilltree based on said equipment is a gameplay loop that kept me entertained for hours on end. The weapons themselves were also really fun, having unique weapon arts which are actually completely viable in all combat scenarios. Mage-hunting and platforming up till the specific boss encounters got enough enjoyment out of me and never overstayed its welcome, even after grinding several mages over and over. With some minor traversal and gravity-related nitpicks, I would still recommend this game. 

The only real shame lies in several equipment pieces being locked into covenant rewards and with the online participation being as dead as it is, they were unobtainable.

Much like its predecessor, the game did not disappoint. Thanks devs, looking forward to next title

I hope it gets more positive feedback so that your work gets the recognition it deserves.

而他真的改了。

如今的体验真的好起来了，第一张和第二张图畅游，数值在第三章有苗头，第四章雪山才开始崩，但相比原先的秒杀级已经优化太多太多了
机械法的手雷伤害还很高，但没有初版逆天
雪山的龙，肉眼可见的变弱了
雷电法和火法不会再直接秒人了
风法的拍皮球还在，容易击飞，但跌落伤害没那么高了。
现在大部分法师的数值不再逆天了，第一个boss耳项链直接成脑瘫了，角色成长曲线平稳多了，绿条回的也快了，消耗品也没缺过。
再开新档直至通关，到圣树之心那才不小心摔死一次。地图里也分了大节点传送，虽然没有升级加点，但是坐火法师进度保留，野生法师追几次后也有追踪（虽然不能一开始就追），经过诸多改动后，我找到了点盐1开荒的感觉，快乐起来了。
但是
DLC
你做的是什么？
当我打完四象的时候，我残存的记忆又浮现在眼前
当我打完DLC最终boss后，22年吃屎的记忆彻底重回我的脑海
本来所谓好了伤疤忘了疼，想给个好评
结果小怪逆天抓取，我搁天上都能让我下来比划比划，大刀冲刺攻击根本不敢冲，只要出抓取动作就有判定，连着抓，抓到快放开键盘等死了。
怪物逆天追击，大老远跳过来，跳boss房里和你妈四象共舞，尤其是冲刺，逢冲，中必倒
地图里逆天激光，其实都可以，怪冲刺攻击冲到我就倒，几只怪一起攻击一起冲，冲就倒，数值也高，心态也搞，♥♥，你设计的是你🐎
蓝黄球那个怪更是重量级，攻击带击退击倒，高追踪高频率，怪还多，几个怪塞起来出手机会一点也没有，我甚至可以预测到两秒以后的死亡😄
开个门隔墙还有仇恨给我秒了
又开始逆天数值逆天判定逆天怪物设计
就四象这boss，你觉得你设计的很幽默？
整个DLC打完，我又回想起开荒时，被当皮球踢，重甲被秒杀，逆天抓取抱摔，满图找法师，被怪堆秒，被闪电秒，被火焰秒，被旋风连，被水球连，被镰刀秒，被手雷秒，被陷阱秒，被无休尊者复读砸地气笑，被隐蔽法无限剑气气笑，被满图炫光气笑，被龙喷火打剩一丝，被土法秒，被小怪各种传球，被弱诱导法术秒，被不死蠕虫随机喷火复读气笑，我没办法，我真的没办法，滚也是死不滚也是死，就卡在那里了。二周目通关后开新档，开修改把绿点满后发现战斗还是一样缺绿，每当回想起这些，我就知道：

I've played this for a while now, and while yes, it's not really a Salt and Sanctuary 2, it still is fun in its own right.

So hear me out for a bit.
Salt and Sanctuary is among my top 5 favourite games, Sacrifice? not quite, but it is a game that I enjoy and would see myself playing with other equipment sets, as I did with Sanctuary.
So yeah, I'd agree that this didn't need to be directly linked to Sanctuary, as it probably made people think that it would be the same gameplay wise, which it isn't. At it's core it is a Soulslike yeah, but the inventory and Monter Hunter elements are what make it different, instead of a single fully connected world that you explore, you have a hub area, from which you can go to 5 (At least as far as I've seen, haven't beaten the game yet, but I'm close) Areas, which I'd say are love or hate, I'm fond of them.

So here I'll list things that were in Sanctuary but won't be here
- Sanctuaries as a whole, NPCs are found in areas and only then do they come to the main hub
- Since there are no Sanctuaries, there are no Creeds or Religions.
- AIR DASH (smh on this one)
- Salt Charged enemies after you die to them
- Weapon Transmutation
- Standard Parry ( Changed for a "block just as you're being hit" way less op imo)
- And other stuff I can't recall atm

Now, stuff that Sacrifice introduces (That you might consider good)
- Actual Online (Co-op, Invasions, Temporal Help, PVP stuff, lots of ways to go about it)
- Greater inventory slots, and 2 new equipment slots for a necklace and knife
- Better (in my opinion) Ranged Weapon System ( Ranged weapons use their own slots, and are not shot until you press the attack button, also more variety on the kinds of weapons)
- Major Focus on Elemental Damages
- Running (If you run outside of combat it doesn't consume stamina, this small detail is so delightful, because I've never seen it before)
- Option of setting a checkpoint without resetting the enemies
- Storage for items


In conclusion, is Sacrifice a bad game? No, I don't think so.
Does it pale in comparison to Sanctuary? Yes, the tones feel completely different, and Sanctuary made more of an impact.
But as stated before, the gameplay differs quite a bit from Sanctuary, so within its own right, it is a fun a cool game.


All things said though, I do want a Sanctuary 2, hope the folks at Ska Studios don't forget about the old ways, if they want to make another one of these, that's fine by me, but just, also keep the Dishwasher/Sanctuary like Games please.

TL:DR Try it at least on discount
I kept hearing terrible stuff about this game and how bad it was but really, it did feel like a worthy sequel.
Biggest pros:
-Difficulty is just right, hard but rewarding
-Enemy variety was awesome
-NPC story arcs were cool and I did feel like I almost had teammate (I played solo, didn't touch multiplayer)
-Cool boss fights and boss designs

Biggest cons:
-The mages regenerated way too much health before you battled them for real
-Some of the mechanics surrounding weapons were a little confusing
-Every level was a bit too small for my liking, the maps were not as immersive as the first game
-The player character looks less cool than in Sanctuary, they were way cuter in the first game

It was a little longer and quite challenging but the biggest issue in the game is the regeneration the mages possess. All these negative reviews confuse me, the game is great!

Well made game in same universe as the first one. Lore is even darker, combat is evolved comparing to the first game and become more versatile. Dex builds now not only viable but even enjoyable. Magic playthrough, even though I haven't finished one yet, also seems promising. 

The game world feels larger than a first game, however, it's split into several big areas so you can't get from one to another not visiting hub zone. 

Boss fights are more or less on the same level, however, the hunt mechanics and the fact that you may face several bosses at once brought a lot of fun and replayability even w\o NG+ that is also presented in this game. 

I have had my share of fun for the last few days (24-25h of playtime) and will gladly recommend this game.

It's a shame this diamond was hidden for so long in a [strike]garbage pile[/strike] Epic games store.

몬스터헌터, 다크소울, 할로우나이트를 섞은 게임인데, 게임에서 똥내가 오지게 난다. 
1. 맵을 탐험하다, 보스 소환처가 보이면 보스를 소환하고 이미 왔던곳을 한번 쑥 훑어봐야 된다는 점이 정말 개같다. 그러면서 지도 기능은 제공하지도 않고, 지점 간 텔레포트 기능도 제공하지 않는다. 
2. 보스는 매번 일정 체력이 까지면 도망치는데, 이러한 전투가 한두번이여 재밌지, 온맵을 뒤지고다니는 이러한 행위는 10번을 넘게 반복하면 피곤하기만 하다. 
3. 온 맵에 걸쳐나오는 다양한 종류의 함정들은 사람의 내재된 분노를 일으키곤 한다. 또한 맵의 특정 포인트는 존재 의의를 모르겠는 곳이 많다
4. 대부분의 보스들의 패턴이 투사체 발사밖에 없어서 다소 아쉽다.
그점을 제외하고는 타임킬링용으로 그럭저럭 할만하다.

Not been playing too long but I was a big fan of the first game. Although this game is indeed quite a bit different from the first game I still find it really enjoyable. The fun combat and Metroidvania style platforming are back and the animations look and feel much improved upon from the first game. 

The game is similar to the first game in many ways but also much different. Its still a souls like but now there are boss enemies that you have to chase around the map while they spawn enemies around them until you reach a certain point and they stand their ground and fight you and you kill them. I may be in the minority but I honestly have really been enjoying this. Its put a really interesting twist on the side scrolling souls like formula that they really nailed in Sanctuary. 

Personally been having a ton of fun and I recommend it if you like the idea of chasing the boss around and collecting materials from their minions they spawn to make gear and weapons.  I found it really awesome. Been playing some on Co op too and the Co op is really great. 

Got it 35% off during the sale and don't regret it at all. I understand why the reviews are more mixed considering how different the game is from the first one, but at least for me, all the important bits were still there (i.e. gaining souls to level, fun builds and weapons/spells etc..) plus a new twist with the Monster Hunter style boss fights with the mages. 

TL:DR: In my opinion, been a really great game so far. Different then the first for sure but still similar on a fundamental level with some Monster Hunter boss chase elements thrown in. Would definitely recommend though. Especially on sale. Had I bought it at full price it would have still been totally worth it. Thanks Ska Studios for another really fun game.

Bir 2D soulslike-metroidvania delisi olarak bu oyunu da kaçırmadım tabii ki ama her ne kadar oyunu iyi bulsam da ilk oyuna göre gözle görülür şekilde geriye gittiğini düşünüyorum. Öncelikle bosslar ilkine göre çok daha kolay (ya da ben daha tecrübeliyim) ayrıca boss hunt olayı getirmişler ki bossların peşlerinde oradan oraya koşturmak çok yorucu ve sıkıcı. Hiç akılda kalıcı değiller ve iyi itemler için onları grind etme fikri bir oyunun içine nasıl ederim sorusunun cevabı. Bu yüzden de basic itemlere full + basıp ilerleme fikri çok daha cazip geldi bu da neredeyse bütün oyunu aynı adamla oynamama yol açtı. Oyun kötü dizaynı ile beni buna itti. İlk oyuna göre bu oyunu sıradanlaştıran bir diğer husus da parçalı dünya. İlk oyun tek bir açık dünyaydı, haritasızdı, uçsuz bucaksızdı, keşif hissi çok güçlüydü. Aynı zamanda çok korkutucuydu. Bu oyun ise bir kaç farklı bölgeye bölünmüş ve ana base'den oralara ışınlanıp sonuna ulaşıp yeni harita parçasını bulup dönmemizi istiyor. Tekrar geri dönüp bakmak için hiçbir sebebimiz yok. Tüm bölgeleri toplasan ilk oyunun yarısı yapar mı emin değilim. Bunları hepsi birleşince ilk oyundaki heyecanın öldüğünü düşünüyorum. Türü sevene eğlenceli vakit geçirtir ama özel bir yanı yok. Chill takılmak istiyorsanız bunu, terlemek istiyorsanız Salt and Sanctuary'i oynayın

一周目开完全怪图鉴通关了，想了想还是给个好评吧。这个好评很大程度，前作情怀加成。这一作只能说能玩，游戏打折40%才值得入，原价个人认为它不值这个价。游戏体量很大，但味如嚼蜡，很平淡，就像量大管饱的白米饭一样。前作多少记忆犹新的BOSS，微笑皇后，无名神，难言深渊，干尸圣三一，五六年过去了我还记得一清二楚。这作的怪，打完也就打完了，长啥样，记不住，没啥特色。长相最怪异的怪也就一个喧嚣塑像。动作简化的就平砍，翻滚，格挡都不怎么用。动作也像木偶人一样。还有那个比滑翔伞，是真的恶心。评价是无聊时的小品游戏，凑合玩玩吧，真心一般。

The Epic Store exclusivity deal was a slap in the face. Don't do that again, Ska Studios.

With that out of the way, the game is good. Great, even. More than a worthy sequel to Salt & Sanctuary.

There's your metroidvania-style exploration, tool acquisition opening up more exploration, non-linear progression with some backtracking and such. A lot of platforming. Done well, some good challenges and overall interestingly designed areas to traverse, but with that said: Prepare for bullcrap. Some of it optional, much of it not. You will, in all likelihood, be knocked off of high places more than once, which often kills you outright and can potentially cost you a lot of salt (experience) and silver (semi-useless currency), though if it should happen, in fairness, you can always get more of both later if you need it. Salt can be recovered from where you died, unless you die again; silver, you lose half of what you were carrying... so spend it ASAP, after you unlock some merchants, and don't worry about it until then.

There's also your 2D-ified soulsy combat, with blocking, timely block-parries, parry counter-crits (underwhelming in this game, to be honest, and they don't work at all on a lot of enemies) and a lot of rolling away from devastating blows, blasts and traps. Also you can hit things with your own weapon now and then, if you time it right. You die, learn, die, get better, and eventually you die less. Your character gets very incrementally stronger but the slope is as long as it is slow; along your journey, you can grow more than strong enough to beat down whatever you like.

Into this ties the gathering, crafting and upgrading. There are materials used for healing potion charges and ranged weapon ammunition replenishment, kind of monster hunter vibes there. Also you track down bizarre, towering mages, fight them and absorb their essence, while also collecting some crafting and upgrade materials. Loads of different mages, which you chase down, fighting their minions as you go, until they make their final stand. There are non-mage boss battles, but most bosses in the game are mages; there's great variety among them, though, and each brings its own attack patterns and minion types. From mage and minion materials, you craft weapons, armor, accessories... all kinds of neat things that grant a variety of protection types, attribute boosts, special and active abilities. Example: A fiery Highblade (katana) can, with the appropriate character ability learned, ignite its blade in order to apply burning damage over time as it strikes. Combine that with a ring of fast stamina recovery and an amulet of fast climbing, and you begin to see the possibilities.

All in all, the game is a rich and rewarding experience - if you're prepared to go in with the necessary zen mentality of accepting death as part of the cycle of learning and all that.
Highly recommended for fans of the original, or anyone wanting to try a fun, oddball mix of Dark Souls, Monster Hunter and Castlevania spanning several vast, darkly beautiful realms.

Played this game by other means while it was stuck on Epic, promised myself I'd buy it when it finally came to Steam.

Definitely a "not for everyone" kind of game, and I can't even really say "If you liked Salt and Sanctuary, you'll definitely like this."

It's kind of everything thrown into a blender. You have Souls-like combat shoved into 2D, Metroidvania style movement upgrades and progression locks to eventually go more places, and you grind out "bosses" to craft equipment to help you to better grind out more bosses for more equipment.

You'll either love it or hate it, but I'd say it's worth at least giving a try. You should have a good idea if it's for you before the 2 hour window runs out.

Salt and Sacrifice is an ambitious expansion on the style and gameplay of Salt and Sanctuary, and the developers handled it well! The formula feels new and innovative without losing the core gameplay that I loved so much in the first game, and with the recent balance update, the difficulty feels fair. The boss fights are hectic, with a real sense of spectacle, and their craftable weapons feel powerful. The level design is a little too sprawling for my tastes, with the main “worlds” not connected, but it doesn’t detract from the overall experience. Fun game! I would recommend playing it on couch co-op for maximum chaos.

UPDATE: I finished the game. Twice. And I LOVED IT. Everything feels really good, from the combat to the exploration (I think there are still some secrets in the DLC I haven't found yet) to the progression, and I actually enjoy the PvP. In a soulslike game! The chaotic 6-player multiplayer is one of the reasons I'm still playing this game, even after finishing it and trying a lot of the weapons and builds. Bang-up job with the game, Ska, and you have my utmost respect for sticking with it and fixing all the fixable mechanical problems people were complaining about. I look forward to whatever you decide to make next!

Salt And Sacrifice. Та игра что действительно сподвигла меня написать на себя отзыв.

Что-ж, начну с того, что игру нет смысла сравнивать с Sanctuary, так-как "Жертва" сильно отличается от неё, как по атмосфере, так и по геймдизайну. 
Но небольшая схожесть в виде геймплея всё-эе присутствует.


Теперь же перейду к самой игре.

И лучше начать с минусов, которые я приметил за всё время игры..
1. Баги с моделями.
Иногда пытаясь зацепится за платформу, персонаж банально проваливался под неё, вместо зацепа. 
2. Несбалансированность мобов и боссов, по отношению к этапам игры где они находятся. 
Войдя в мир после создания персонажа, разобравшись в основах и получения первого символа вместе с допуском в первый акт мы можем увидеть то, что начальные вроде как мобы бьют очень то даже не сильно, но после убийства первого же обучающего босса, начинается провал в балансировке. 
Взяв первый контракт на именного мага, появляются зомби в повязках, что мало того респавняться если отойти за экран, так и бьют больнее чем сам первый маг. Дальше же, после убийства первых трёх именных магов, а именно (Огонь,Мороз,Вода), Маг тока, начинает снимать безбожно много здоровья за попадание, так еще и будучи почти недоступным для Мили-билдов к атаке. И такая ситуация на каждой локации, что правда после освоения механики боя перестает быть проблемой.

3. Баланс же оружия в пвп, сводится к заточке твоего противника и скейлинга по твоему уровню прогресса, потому не стоит будучи непрокачаненым лезть в это.

Перейду к плюсам.
1. На удивление живой онлайн, благодаря кроссплатформе. И скейлинг персонажей к уровню нашего.
2. Для любителей гринда предоставлены все условия к этому.
3. Довольно интересные Маги и боссы.

В целом описывать прохождение игры нет никакого смысла. 
Для любителей соулс-лайков что не бояться 2D вида игры, самое то. Благо Бабадзаки приучил нас терпеть:3

All the worst BS of Sanctuary here is core gameplay, now with added salt.

- One shots
- HP sponges
- Inflated damage
- Unavoidable AOEs, stuns, grabs, and chains of stuns /grabs
- Unavoidable spamming of AOEs, stuns, grabs, and chains of stuns /grabs
- Unavoidable juggles and spamming of juggles (even in heavy sets)
- Chained juggling/ grabs/ stuns/ AOEs + all of the above and below resulting in unavoidable death

- Mages spamming unavoidable spells / AOEs
- Mobs and Mages spamming juggles
- Mobs and Mages spamming stuns
- Mages spamming summons that prevent damage
- Said summons being covered by the now immortal mage
- Running around in slow mo trying to chase said summons while all of the above and below goes on

- Enemies jumping 3/4 of the screen
- Enemies jumping 3/4 of the screen through solid walls
- Enemies floating / flying all over the screen and attacking you through solid walls (for real)
- Enemies now being invisible because they are floating / flying through "black" solid walls, all the while attacking you with spells or melee, while all of the above and below goes on
- Bonus: Enemies can juggle you through rolls and walls

- Enemies have infinite stamina and projectiles/ fp
- Enemies stuns, grabs, and juggles have no cooldown 
- Enemies stuns, grabs, and juggles can be chained by single enemies or groups
- Enemies can block 100% of damage (minus 2 statuses that deal low damage over time)
- Enemies will chase you across the whole map if they can pathfind a way, and chase you over other aggressors
- A bat bites you for 1/4 of your HP at level 55 and wearing a full heavy set (like, wtf)

- Your character moves like underwater, and the world is on speed (regardless of weight)
- Clunky controls: you try to "jump down" to get on a lower platform, but you jump up, possibly into a trap...GG
- Drinking a potion takes way too long for way too little gains + sitting duck syndrome
- Your auto-targeting abilities can and will prioritise the mob half a screen away / entirely out of screen rather than the boss mauling your pretty face off
- Rolling has both less frames and range than incoming attacks / chains of attacks, do your math

- Enemies that guard ledges and hit you as you try to land/ grab on them so you fall to unavoidable death
- Enemies that teleport you into burning chairs that deal high damage (as the hanging men is the older game, but worse due to mob gank squads)
- You cannot "aim" at an enemy, the first one in line on a 2D plane gets the bullet

- Later weapons (tier 3+) may have way worse skills than early/ mid-game variations, i.e. projectiles that will very rarely hit an enemy and for insignificant damage
- This is especially bad considering the whole game is based on heavy grinding, and it fails to reward the player for the time invested
- Heavy weapons force you to trade in a game where trading hits is a one-way ticket to stunlock-landia and reload-screen-o-landia

- A number of cheats for enemies and limitations that are only applicable to the player

- Dead PVE / PVP in a game -poorly- balanced around multiplayer (i.e. aggro management and pal revives)

- Chasing mages through multiple sections of trap-filled, ganking-mobs infested, and bad platforming-heavy levels gets very old very fast; especially when each of these can and will one-shot you one way or the other + all of the above goes on, resulting in unavoidable death

- So much deaf and relentless obnoxiousness that you won't stop playing for any other reason that you just don't care, you are not having fun, and you would rather do anything else, including the dishes

Finished Sanctuary and still enjoy it after 50+ hours. 
Here, I stopped having fun in 15 already.
It's like they won't listen the community no matter what.
Do you know the term "bait and switch"? You get five, maybe ten decent hours, then player trolling without irony.
Quite possibly the worst experience I had in 30+ years of gaming (and I am a Souls enthusiast). It's not that the game is just "hard" (although all of the above doesn't make it easy); it's that is pure tedium and BS unending.
It's a game where RNG is left unchecked, no cooldowns, no logic, so, rather than learning, you are getting either more or less lucky (oh, I got juggled/ grabbed through a roll/ one-shotted/ thrown to unavoidable death, ok, maybe next time I won't).
I would like SKA to fix it, but you can't fix stupid and I cannot find another cause for the shameless mess I am putting up with.
Since I bought it, I may as well try to power through and update my review; but you please learn from it.

Following the previous beloved tale of "Sodium Deficient Dude Kills God"...

We join yet another severely sodium deprived dude who has gotten so desperate to get their fix, they decide to commit a random crime, and because they are sodium deprived, they screw it up and get caught. The penalty is one of two options: enlist in special forces, or die. Quite the difficult choice, there...

Our protagonist chooses the former, and their mission is then given to them: There's a bunch of mages screwing up the ecosystem! They are screwing it up so bad the game has become less of a soulslike and more of a Monster Hunter World-like! So it is our protagonist's job to.....KILL EVERYTHING THAT MOVES!

As our hero/heroine dwindles the numbers of mages, the gods that are in charge of developing this world suddenly realize they were supposed to make a soulslike, and crank the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ meter to 11 as you approach endgame! We're talking juggling, one hit kills, quick moving bosses demanding precision reactions, and more move spamming than a Down-B Kirby in Smash Bros.

And then you kill Gwyn and either link the flame and make the gods feel special or kill it and piss them off something fierce. GG.

I don't think it's horrible, but I also came in knowing it was not going to be very faithful to the original. (Thanks previous steam reviewers!) If you want my honest opinion, it's an acquired taste, but it at least warrants one playthrough. Just to, at least somewhat, scratch that itch that Salt and Sanctuary left us with.

Rating: Dark Souls II/10

从2.0之后才开始玩的，应该是修复了很多问题，也降低了难度，体验还不错，和一代比较体验类似，不像其他很多评价说的那么差。
目前一周目全收集打完，包括新增的冠军之乡dlc，因为离线完玩一些时间，实际游戏时长大约25h，后续估计会打到20+周目，到时候再补充高周目的体验。

-----------后续补充--------------------------
6周目通关了，在6周目把全boss打了一遍，感觉高周目人物护甲就是纸糊的，体验较差，机关多打中一两下就必死，被小怪连中几下必死，部分boss一招反应慢了直接死，怀念一周目穿上化圣套之后的硬度。
个人经验，负重需要压在50%以下翻滚速度才够快，不然DLC的boss原初受印人几乎打不过。
----------再后续----------
通关了N+10，应该是从NG+9以后难度不变了，因为NG+9和+10的最终boss给的经验差不多，感觉已经腻了，有更新的话再回来玩，小怪和陷阱比价头疼，很容易被秒；boss反而很好打，变招不多，而且boss很少秒人。

As a big fan of Salt & Sanctuary (played multiple runs all in co-op with my partner), I was initially a bit worried hearing that Salt & Sacrifice was apparently a bit different. I am very pleased to say that it's an incredible sequel, a very interesting blend of Soulslike (similar to the first) and Monster Hunter (a series I have no experience with).

In my time with the game so far I'm having a blast, the systems are engaging and the loop is very enjoyable. Other reviews made me think it would be a massive departure from the first, and it is different, but there's less different than I was expecting. Overall, progression isn't tightly tied to Mage Hunts anyway, so there's no mandated grind.

If you liked the first, I think you'll have a lot of fun with this one! Keep in mind though, I'm playing with online co-op (which has been flawless), and that has added a lot of fun as we discuss builds and where to explore next. Some bosses might be less enjoyable solo, but going hunting Mages together & exploring has been really great!

Wishing all the best to the developers, thank you for a great game :)

由于评论区看有不少打零点几小时就开喷来故意拉低游戏整体质量的，这里从我个人角度来评价盐2，内容仅供参考作为参考，不喜勿喷：

首先这游戏早之前我是在epic上面玩过的，后来上架Steam后为凑齐ska大全套忍下心多花五六十又买一次（挺后悔买太早了~）；为了防止个别黑粉怀疑我啥也不懂故意为该游戏拉好评在此郑重说明一下：盐1我是今年暑假前开始玩的，除深渊和天火跑图以外已经打十来周目了;二代之前epic上的档目前四周目恐石峰刚打完时法。

个人感觉二代风评不好主要就是核心剧情玩法偏离一代+缝合怪（但这不是最主要的），小怪自身能力确实很强以及个别地方地形杀严重导致推图过程中非常容易暴毙。然后是boss和法师战，这里点名批评阴影法，就那出招速度贼快堪比疯狗一般，难度相当于盐1十个大湖加一起都抵不上的那种（自己体会吧，我是真打不过），风法血法还有其他boss（前期绿猎手和无休尊者除外）真心表示不算太难，只要背会出招节奏基本上都是勉强能打过的，除非你考虑多周目继续受苦受难攒成就或有其他想法的。

最后对我而言这游戏最严重最得改的就是角色耐力和前摇设计，二代攻击翻滚和格挡等耗精都贼拉高，而且角色受击和浮空期间不会回复耐力，回血动作和起身等前摇太长导致战斗期间刚爬起又一波带走的情况持续发生，间接导致整体操控手感和灵活度没有盐1那种爽感很不舒服。希望这点官方需要重视并重新调整一下，不然针对各别出手较快的大怪小怪非常容易吃瘪影响整个游戏的体验感。

总之一句话，这游戏你们觉得好就觉得好，觉得烂不是错，错就错在个别黑子明知故犯，故意花几十块钱稍微玩个几分钟就打差评恶意拉低分数抹黑造谣，不喜欢就别买免得招人嫌影响人心情毕竟还有其他人在玩；如果实在想玩又想玩好的建议先从网上多看几遍实况解说再来考虑除非你对你自己的实力足够自信。

（PS：顺带提一下这个游戏是支持跨平台的，也就是说你在epic上玩的时候留的旧档在Steam上照样可以正常打开，但是成就不共享只能二周目或重开一个档把里面所有东西重新刷一遍）

TL;DR
The game's good.
But takes some getting used to.

I'll get this out of the way first, this isn't a Sanctuary 2 like I know a lot of us where hoping it'd be. And if you go into this game right after Sanctuary, you'll get hit with a learning curve. It's not steep, but it is noticeable. And I'll just say, gone are they days of face-♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ the big monster bosses to death before the second phase starts.


I'll get the meanest issue I had out of they way first.
Mage-Fights.
Most of what you'll be fighting boss-wise are the mages, and you'll be fighting A LOT of them of all shapes and sizes.
Unfortunately, while they all look different, they each follow the same pattern of spawning a few minions for you to kill, before running away a couple times before the real boss-fight starts, which's where the monster-hunter aspect you may've heard about comes into play. And the most thinking you'll do in in these "chase phases" of each fight, is leading other monsters/mages in to pull aggro from the main mage you're hunting. But that's about it.
Unlike most of their scripted, non-mage counterparts there isn't much strategy to fighting the mages themselves. Other than keep dodging or blocking attacks until there's a gap in their spell-spam. And if possible exploit their elemental weakness.
Killing the mages and their minions gets you your materials for elemental themed weapons and gear. And one of the few aspects I actually like from fighting the mages. You'll get a bit of dialogue from each one before they truely die. Ranging form angry last words, to crumbs of the overall story.

The other thing that really grated on my nerve was the platforming. It all works 99% of the time, but ever now and then you won't grab a ledge, the grappling hook won't grapple, or that stupid sail-cloth won't work and you'll plummet to your death... This was an issue in Sanctuary too. Fall damage in 2D games is always annoying, andIt's upsetting this wasn't addressed in some way. (At least the wall-jump is built in from the start this time)
Especially when your platforming around those ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ green knights, that can jump almost anywhere and EVERY hit from their glaives send you flying! >:(
♥♥♥♥ the Mireguard, I'm glad their stupid swamp was destroyed!

Also there's still no dedicated map mechanic, and more that once I've had to google one to remember a quicker route somewhere.


With the negative stuff out of the way, I'll move on to what I actually like in the game

Exploring each area from top to bottom is just as, if not more so rewarding than before. Knowing the terrain you'll be fighting on when hunting mages can at times give you an advantage over them. And there's enough landmark-like locations so you're less likely to get lost. That said, a map will defiantly help with subsequent playthroughs in getting to latter stages faster.

Combat's just as enjoyable as before, and the dedicated ranged weapons are a nice change. No longer will you have to lug around a bow in your second weapons slot just to pull enemies out one at a time.
Early game magic isn't limited to just fire and lightning spells. And if you see a mage use a spell, chances are YOU'LL be able to use that same spell with one of the weapons you craft from the materials they drop.
So all and all, the weapons are way more diverse, and fun to play around with.

Wish I could say the same for armor...
The old system is still here, and heavy armor is almost always more of a hinderance than it's light counterpart. Not taking hit-stun with poise is cool and all, but avoiding damage altogether is just always going to feel better. And this next point is a nit-pick, but design wise I don't care for most of the new armors. Fashion-Souls this is not.
I suspect this's due to the new PvP aspects of the game, and being able to know exactly what your up against when you get invated/hop into the arena. But I can't say that for sure.

This one is small, but the inclusion of more NPC's dotted around the explorable zones in a nice touch. Even if most of them are PvP faction people. Really makes the world you're in feel alive. And a place you're actively fighting to save. Even if in typical souls-like fashion, all seems lost.

Story-wise it runs the same vein as Sanctuary. Bits of lore scattered though item/enemy descriptions, and short story segments in the skill tree. There's a few things hinted at  in the Traitor's Tomb "DLC" area that came out recently that may or may not elude to your created-character not just being some random criminal off the street. But it's nothing concrete, and you're meant to draw your own conclusions about it.


Overall, if you liked Sanctuary you'll probably like Sacrifice. 
But be warned, it's not a direct sequel,  there's less Dark-Souls in the formula this time around, and the grind for certain weapons/skills/equipment is real.

Also don't ask my about the PvP/Online stuff. I haven't bothered with it, and don't plan to.
That ♥♥♥♥'s for try-hards.
An improvement in combat and gameplay compared to the original, though the story feels weaker.

[h1]A Prime Example Of "Mashing Genres Together Won't Automatically Make Things Better"[/h1]

I fail to understand the thought process behind making one of the best indie souls-like into a monster-hunter-like. Actually, the game is labeled as "Monster-Hunter-like, Souls-like Metroidvania". I think to myself, maybe the developers find a way to mingle the merits from the three of them. After all, Salt And Sanctuary is a great souls-like metroidvania and probably the pioneer of this sub-genre. The result begs to differ, negatively. Salt and Sacrifice is a chimera. Developers borrow a lot of core ideas from the three genres, stitch them together, and somehow fail to breathe life into it. It's a shame because I really want to like this game. I like the iconic ska studio art style, and the animation is way smoother than Salt And Sanctuary. Everything else ruins the whole experience.

[h1]Things I Like About Salt And Sacrifice:[/h1]

[b]Minor Compliments[/b]
→The iconic ska art style.
→I like the mages and their lackeys' design.
→Unique and fancy weapons, armors and trinkets to craft.
→I guess Perfect Block is now a new standard for souls-like.
→Seeing mages fighting each other is oddly satisfying.

[h1]Things I Dislike About Salt And Sacrifice:[/h1]

[b]1. The Chimera Problem[/b]
Like I said before, mashing genres together won't automatically make the game fun to play. You need to have the right mechanic and design to glue the features together.
→[b]In terms of monster-hunter-like[/b]: first, hunting mages does not feel good. Instead of you giving it all to take down a powerful being, the hunt plays out more like fighting the lackeys it summons until the mage stops running. The whole process before the showdown feels like a slog designing to stretch the length of the fight as much as possible; second, there is no real "hunting" involved. You find the residue the mage left behind, you accept the mission, and then you follow the guiding smoke till you find the culprit. No tracking. No collecting information. Just say yes to the mission and you are set; last, the most important part which was left behind, skills and set bonus each armor provides. I know the traditional level system and skill tree exist, but the lacking of those build varieties is such a missed opportunity to encourage players to hunt more mages and try out different build combinations.
→[b]In terms of metroidvania[/b]: This is not even a downgrade, Salt And Sacrifice straight up ditches the level design in favor of the monster-hunter-like gameplay. None of the great design from previous title can be seen here. There is no actual mobility to unlock, and naturally there is no platforming or bosses' attacks to incentivize players to use those abilities more. The so-called gating abilities in this game are all just glorified keys to unlock shortcuts or activate hidden platforms. This issue won't be such hassle if the level design can properly use them. Alas, it doesn't.
→[b]In terms of souls-like[/b]: this is more of a rant about design philosophy rather than gameplay mechanic. Often time when other games try to imitate the magic of the Souls-series, they always focus on the gameplay only. Of course gameplay is a core feature, but other things are just as important. Coherent item descriptions, logical enemy placements, environmental story-telling, the list goes on and on. Somehow Salt And Sacrifice throws everything away. Item descriptions of the equipment provides nothing vital clues or some interesting story. Most of them feel like they were assembled by random word generator. Bosses have no identity at all since they don't even drop their equipment when they're defeated, therefore no lore to read through. The Mages do not fare well either. Despite the cool design, their roaming nature and the lacking of dedicated levels make them more of elite mobs rather than significant bosses. 

[b]2. Other Complaints[/b]
→Locking all the weapon ability behind one or two stat requirement is not a good idea.
→Collecting resource to craft health potion does not fit souls-like.
→Enemies late-game deal absurdly high damage.
→The geyser/glider ability feels awkward to use.
→A proper map will make navigating the world less confusing and frustrating.

This game is not Salt and Sanctuary 2. It doesn't even feel like a game made by the same creators. They took a good formula for a fun game and tried to turn it into an MMO evidently, because that's what this game feels like. Sacrifice isn't a soulslike, it's a grindy mess that doesn't seem like it knows what it wants to be, lazily reused mage bosses, and removed multiple features from Sanctuary that made the gameplay feel smooth. Upgraded combat from Sanctuary, mix in some Monster Hunter mechanics, and a cool new storyline.

Nice game, doesn't deserve the hate it's getting. It's not Salt & Sanctuary 2, so don't go in expecting it to be. Still mainly a Souls-like with the combat and build making systems being pretty much the same as in Sanctuary, but with a few Hunter-like elements thrown in. Sanctuary might be 2D Dark Souls, but this one isn't 2D Monster Hunter, not at all, so don't go in expecting that either. It's its own thing, a bit experimental I'd say, but it turned out well.

[h3]About the other reviews...[/h3]

A lot of the issues other people complain about have been fixed with the latest update. Or maybe never existed in the first place? Idk, I only started playing recently. Bosses juggling you around isn't really something that happens. There are a few combo attacks, but they usually deal around the same damage in total as a single normal attack.  With some of these reviews I wonder if people even know that you have iframes on rolls, because honestly, they're making it out to be a way bigger deal than it is. The biggest danger is getting stunlocked because multiple enemies attack you at the same time or getting punched off a cliff (although the fall damage is extremely forgiving, you gotta be in free fall for a couple of seconds for it to be lethal). And while multiple enemies can swarm you, they will also fight each other, so just use that to your advantage. 75% of the time enemies will be pre-occupied when you arrive and you can just attack from behind.

[h3]Gameplay[/h3]

As for the gameplay, basically there are 5 different locations, each being a big and open map with various sub-areas that are interconnected like in Sanctuary. But the main locations are disconnected and accessed via a hub, like in Demon's Souls. The exploration is very similar to Sanctuary, with some backtracking to access pathways that require specific tools. There are regular bosses spread throughout each map, but the main combat loop comes from Mage hunts.

The hunts can be divided into 2 parts. First you accept a mission to hunt a specific Mage (there are a total of 21 different types) and proceed to chase it around the map. During this stage the Mage will rarely actively attack you and instead mainly spawn minions. Regular enemies, other Mages and their minions as well as hazeburned enemies (which only spawn while a hunt is active) will all be on the map alongside you and the Mage you're hunting. It's basically a free for all brawl. Once the Mage receives enough damage it will flee to another nearby spot on the map (always an open spot, they never spawn in tight hallways or something, the game has lots of big rooms) and if you repeat this enough times, you'll enter stage 2. The chase phase really isn't too troublesome, there is no time limit and you can even rest at a "bonfire" and restock your "Estus" in the middle of it without losing your progress. In the second stage you'll enter a proper boss fight with the Mage. Once you get close enough to it a health bar will pop up and walls will be erected around the area, so you can't run away or get punched off a platform. This is where the Mage will actively attack you with its full moveset and no longer spawn minions, although if minions or other enemies were present at the beginning of the fight they will stay there (and possibly fight the Mage alongside you). Mages as well as their minions drop materials specific to that kind of Mage, which you use to craft gear, same as in Monster Hunter.

The new DLC adds a 6th area to the game, but outside of a boss rush-style arena (which is an awesome addition) there are no Mages here, it's purely Souls-like in this case. You unlock it pretty early on, but it's kinda divided into 3 sub-areas with big difficulty differences, so I think you're meant to leave it after killing a sub-area boss and returning later once you're stronger. It's not a small DLC either, it has various hidden paths and a total of 4 bosses (one being a secret boss). The setting is probably my favourite in the entire game and it adds to the already very interesting lore. 

[h3]My Verdict[/h3]

Honestly, the only complaint I have about the game is the balancing in terms of raw numbers. At some point, roughly halfway through the game, everything seems to be turning into a glass cannon, both the player and the enemies. You start 1-3 hitting all the mobs and a lot of boss/Mage fights are over within less than a minute, whether you win or lose. Which is a shame, because some of them have cool movesets that would be rewarding to learn and master. But in practise you mostly end up killing them before they get use to all of it, or they kill you ofc. If everything did like half damage, the game would be more fun imo. Hopefully a future balancing patch will change this. Other than that, it's a nice game, with unique gameplay and a banger OST (the Mage theme goes hard). I have done 2 complete playthroughs so far and unlocked every achievement, all of which was a fun time. I definitely plan on making more characters in the future for various different builds as well.

I do still prefer Sanctuary and I do still hope that Ska's next game will be more like that again, but I don't think Sacrifice was a waste of a game. I would also like to see it getting a sequel at some point, ideally with better damage balancing and a bigger grind. Perhaps even higher difficulties/ranks, like in Monster Hunter, instead of NG+, since I found a bunch of fights, particularly the Mages, were too easy (but part of that was the damage you deal ofc).

Salt and Sacrifice is parts of Salt and Sanctuary, and Monster Hunter sandwiched together. With some interesting results.

+Art is unique and nice to look at. 
+Wide variety of bosses, both "Salt" bosses and "Mage" Bosses 
+Soundtrack is okay, minimalist if anything. Creature and sound effects are good.
+Hate it or love it. Salt has a way of ping ponging and throwing you off a cliff or send you flying 50 feet across the screen. Just laugh it off.
+Variety of craftable armour  sets and weapons which are looted from the different mages across the game. Each with their own weights, stats, and art. Scythes, Daggers, Wands, Rods, Swords, Hammers 
+Hub world (I.E Dark Souls 3) , Shortcuts unlocked
+Quite a number of varied classes and an expansive talent tree.

Now for the negatives

-So, mage hunts are a mixed bag. When you start a mage hunt you follow a trail and start attacking the mage, he retreats and you follow along. Eventually you will box it in an arena and have to fight it. The game is usually generous with save points, with a small run back. Hate it or love it i guess is your call.
-Now even while you are on the hunt for a mage, there are other mages that can spawn on the map. This can be both frustrating and chaotic as you can either get dunked or the mages and their minions will attack each other.
-Biggest gripe I have with this system is, when you down a mage he kneels and you get a prompt to hit a button to "assassinate" it. Only issue is if there is an additional interactive element nearby you usually get drawn to that instead of killing the mage. This is a very case select scenario though. Just a small grievance I have.

Overall a different game than Salt and Sanctuary. Much less Dark Souls and Metroidvania, more Monster Hunter and semi linear gameplay route. Still has it's many moments of fun and two endings and new game +

"The game that became Salt and Sanctuary was actually originally intended to be a mage hunting roguelike when I (James) started making some art for it in 2011 or so. I ended up on a Castlevania DS kick and decided it was safer to make something more in that vein, but after Sanctuary's success I figured maybe it was a good time to try making the mage hunting game."

Dear James....eh no....

1- its not just Castlevania. Salt and Sanctuary genius is its inspiration from Dark Souls.

2-It was not "safer" to do a 2d Castlevania / DarkSouls combo - its brilliant.  Roguelike is a trendy shallow concept that combined with the Monster Hunter formula becomes an utter asinine grind chore. Learn ur own craft man - and listen to the community. This game is not in the same league as Salt and Sanctuary.

3-Play some more DS
确实有它可玩的地方，但是在这基础上需要忍受各种不合理的细节，时常让人高血压。看似魂游，实则刷子游戏。
Это как Salt and Sanctuary, но в 100 раз хуже.

Think this is one of those "It's a good game, but not a good *series title* game."

After a bit of play, I can see why Sanctuary fans are disappointed. I think if they just titled this game something else it would have saved them a lot of heartache and allowed more expansion/exploration of mechanics, because a lot of choices do feel anchored down to the idea of being a "Salt" game.

While Sanctuary is more of a Soulslike, Sacrifice is closer to a Metroidvania where you spend more time expanding secrets and traversal options than you do exploring a vast-feeling interconnected world. With that said there is a bit of an identity crisis because you can feel they weren't entirely willing to commit to being a Metroidvaina so it sits awkwardly in the middle of the two. This wouldn't be as big of an issue if it weren't that the game is still advertised as being the former. 

If you're on the fence, give it a shot but go in with the expectation that this is NOT a real sequel to Salt and Sanctuary.

Salt and Sanctuary with Monster Hunter boss chasing mechanics.

Having the ability to run is a nice and welcome change coming from Sanctuary. Removing magic and giving a few abilities to weapons is nice for those who don't normally play magic builds.

Chasing after the mages will get tedious and annoying when you are already dealing with zone enemies/traps and summons from said mages. Enemy damage is all over the place with random mobs/bosses doing insane damage despite having fully upgraded armor while other mobs in the same zone barely doing damage at all. 

Game is decent, the online pvp/co-op is a more fun experience compared to Sanctuary.
Honestly, a wonderful game and great fun. The monster hunter aspect added into the game adds a lot of fun to the level exploration and game-play.

Skip this one. I totally loved the first game but everywhere the first game succeeded this game failed. The monster hunter aspect of the game feels like they just copied over the mechanics haphazardly but did not have enough time to playtest them to see if they were actually fun. If you are looking for more Salt and Sanctuary, you are going to have to just go play that game again because you will not find that quality here.
This game is great. I personally prefer this to salt and sanctuary and love almost everything about this game. Expect for the fire temple of fire that place sucked. 8/10 - 2 points fire temple.

-/+Rozgrywka – To bardzo udane połączenie trzech gatunków Souls-like'a (albo Slasher'a jeśli sztucznie wyłączymy staminę) Monster Huntera i Metroidvani bo : Ogólny postęp jest zorganizowany jak w Metroidvani a gra się w to jak Souls-like'a/Slashera 2D ale też na każdej mapie mamy 4-5 „Magów” czyli MH'owych potworów do ubicia i może że brzmi to dziwnie ale bardzo dobrze ze sobą współgra i tworzy jedną zwartą całość. A poza tym ostatnia lokacja jest nie proporcjonalnie trudniejsza od pozostałych.

+Walka – Typowa Slasher'owa rzeźnia w stylu SKA Studio serio przez jakieś dwie pierwsze lokacje grałem z „włączoną” staminą i w ogóle tego nie czułem. Broni jest mnóstwo od sztyletów przez miecze, topory, bronie miotelne, drzewcowe różnej maści, a kończąc Odachi, Wielkich Mieczach czy toporach i ogromnych młotach oraz mamy jeszcze trzy różne typy broni magiczniej. Czytaj każdy znajdzie coś dal siebie. Każda z rodzajów broni ma swoje unikalne komba i specjalne ataki. Przeciwników i „Magów” też jest zatrzęsienie i co niespotykane nie ma tu recyklingu przeciwników ta w ogóle czytaj nie znajdziecie dwóch nawet podobnych wrogów... wow po prostu wow... a no i dodajmy do tego system podatności i odporności na pięć podstawowych żywiołów i ich połączenia. Jest SUPER !

-/+Fabuła – Cóż niby coś tam jest ale można całkowicie to pominąć, więc... typowe Soulsy.

+Grafika – SKA Studio ma swój nie podrabiany styl graficzny. Tutaj też się go trzymają dlatego gra wygląda ślicznie i brzmi bardzo dobrze, nawet nie przeszkadza mi to że dźwięki to asety używane od początku ich twórczości kiedy jeszcze tworzyli wspaniałe Slasher'y w stylu DMC albo Bayonetty. (Polecam obadać XD) 

W ogólnym rozrachunku : To wspaniały miszmasz gatunkowy który zadowoli fana nawet tylko jednego z wymienionych wyżej rodzajów gier, BA ! Może być to świetna okazja żeby w kontrolowanym środowisku czytaj gatunku którym czujemy się mocni sprawdzić czy odpowiada nam inny typ rozgrywki np. Monster Hunter w swoje bardzo podstawowej formie.
Gorąco polecam każdemu fanowi Souls-like'ów/Slasher'ów i/lub Monster Hunterów i/lub Metroidvani. A nuż może odkryjecie kolejny ulubiony gatunek 9/10

Ahoi liebe Leute, 

Salt and Sacrifice ist ein Spaßiges und Gelungenes Spiel im Salt and Sanctuary Universum. 
Es grenzt sich allerdings mit anderen Gameplay von Salt and Sanctuary ab und man sollte es nicht als Nachfolger betrachten. Das ist es nämlich nicht sondern ein Spinoff. 

Die schlechten Bewertungen rühren auch daher das diese Leute ein Salt and Sanctuary 2 erwartet haben, was man aber nicht bekommt und daher die Enttäuschung für sie Darstellt. 
Kann ich verstehen wird dennoch den Game nicht gerecht, mich haben die vielen Schlechten Bewertungen sogar verunsichert. 
Ich bin allerdings sehr froh das ich den Game eine Chance gegeben habe weil mir Gefällt es. 

Aber wenn es kein Salt and Sanctuary 2 ist, was ist es dann ? 
Am Besten lässt es sich als ein Crossover aus Salt and Sanctuary und Monster Hunter umschreiben.

Wir sind ein Inquisitor der dazu verdammt ist, ein von Bösen Magiern heimgesuchtes Land von diesen zu befreien. Wir besuchen einzelne in sich geschlossenen Karten und jagen da unterschiedliche Magier über die Karte bis wir sie in ein Finalen Kampf bekämpfen und schlagen.
Die Geschlagenen Magier und ihre Minions lassen Ressourcen fallen die wir zum craften neuer Ausrüstung nutzen können. Der Grind dabei ist nicht so Heftig wie in Monster Hunter. 

Jeder einzeln dieser Magier wir eingeführt durch eine Jagd auf einen "Namenhaften Magier" haben wir diesen geschlagen so treffen wir die "Namenlosen Magier" an, diese können im Grunde das selbe wie ihre Namenhaften Kollegen dienen aber zum weiteren Farmen von Ressourcen. 
Neben den Magiern gibt es auf jeder Karte auch noch weitere Bosskämpfe gegen diverse Zwischen-Bosse.

So prügeln wir uns durch die einzelnen Level und schalten Stück für Stück weitere Bereiche dieser Orte frei oder finden Runen für das Freischalten weitere Level. 
Starten tut unsere Reise immer von einer Hub wo wir diverse Händler und anderer NPCs antreffen, ähnlich wie Majula in Dark Souls 2. Über ein Portal mit Runen "Wählscheibe" reisen wir in die anderen Level.
Man kehrt auch immer wieder in die einzeln Level zurück sobald man neue Fähigkeiten für die weitere Erkundung freigeschaltet hat oder falls man bestimmt Ressourcen sucht. 
Mir gefällt dieser Gameplay Loop und besonders der unkomplizierte "Drag and Drop" Multiplayer sagt mir sehr zu. Dieser ermöglicht es uns, falls wir mal ein Boss nicht schaffen, andere Mitspieler für diesen Kampf anzuwerben. Diese bekommen dann auch etwas von der Beute, zudem kann man auch ähnlich wie in Dark Souls geraidet werden, was aber nur passiert wenn wir einen Mitspieler bei uns haben. 
Man kann also auch beruhigt im Singelplayer Leveln und Looten ^^

Ebenfalls gefällt mir Besonders der Art - Stil : Rüstungen und Gegner/Bosse schauen Prima aus und man hat für jeden Geschmack etwas. 
Es gibt Schwere und Leichte Rüstungen, diverse Waffen Gattungen und ein Elemente System was eine nette "Stein, Schere und Papier" Dynamik ins Spiel bringt. 
Unserer Gegner haben verschiedenen Element Typen und diese gilt es mit den Passenden Loadout zu kontern um uns den Kampf zu erleichtern. 
Nach meinen Empfinden ist es ein wenig Einsteiger Freundlicher und "Leichter" als Salt and Sanctuary, was aber nicht heißt das es einfach ist. 
Zusätzlich sollte man erwähnen das es keine Karte gibt, was ein Hindernis sein kann da die Levels teils ganz schön verschachtelt sind. Dies tut aber auch kein Abbruch und reizt zumindest bei mir den Entdecker Drang. 

Kauft euch das Spiel nicht wenn ihr Salt and Sanctuary 2 erwartet.
Aber wenn ihr diese Universum liebt so wie ich und offen für etwas neues seid, dann solltet ihr den Game eine Chance geben.

Soviel zu meinen Eindruck zu Salt and Sacrifice !

Downgrade in basically every aspect. Mages hunting is a good idea done in a terrible way and since game relies a lot of hunting, many hours were just filled with pain. Disappointing.
只想過一個結局，遊戲即便過了一年還是能感覺到黑暗靈魂2的疊怪跟惡意，簡單的說，玩家處於魂系的遊戲中，小怪跟王不是在無雙就是在格鬥遊戲連段中，而且真的追王追得非常疲倦。

Since the epic games launch this game has substantially improved in a lot but it still suffers from an identity crisis. 
It's a fusion of a Souls like and a Monster hunter like title ; in itself thats not the worst thing, but while trying to do both things equally it makes for an obtuse experience.

Mages attack patterns seem wildly inconsistent with very high damage and knockback attached where even the toughest armor still sees you losing half your health to something the mage easily filled the screen with and is still doing. Due to the gameplay loop being following mages across awkwardly small and perilous stages until you arrive at a small arena to be quickly filled with attacks , projectiles, roll catches-you name it. All mages feel about the same with little variety, which is a shame and a let down from both angles of souls like and MH-like.

Chase sequence take you through Souls-like maps which means filled with traps, lethal falls and high damage mobs; in later areas some mages will run away past a jump puzzle and once you reach it; it will run away back through it. When youre not dealing with hunting a mage, the maps themselves are decent the only issue with them is due to the comically strong knock back a lot of things have , youre often having to do that climb you just did all over again.

Oddly enough the most well designed fights that make for the better experience are not the mages, but the other non mage bosses such as the dragon. Having a dedicated stage thats spacious or having attack patterns that are telegraphed and have space between themselves to breath makes for a fun experience- who wouldve guessed.

Edit-The Final boss did play a different track this time? I had gone back to help a friend for the other ending and this time it wasnt the default mage theme. Probably a bug; leaving that bit in just incase someone else runs into it
Music is practically nonexistent ; Mages have 1 shared track which the final boss also uses.. Other bosses also have one shared track, most of the time you spend your time in awkward silence or very faint ambiance. Exceptions are the final map and a section of the new one they added in do have dedicated tracks but its strange seeing such a drastic drop in variety from the maker of Dishwasher series or Charlie murder. Its understandable wanting a more unsettling ambiance focused track for the tone and setting of the game- Monster Hunter World did not have constant tracks playing for every map for example; but that did have different battle themes for each map for small and big enemies alike- as well as a short intro theme for day and night- i had wished something similar was implemented as a break away from the unnerving silence.

I want to love this game, SKA and specifically James Silva has made my favorite games and favorite sound tracks , and maybe this didnt have to be Salt and Sanctuary 2 or even similar; its own thing as he clearly wanted it to be- but i do wish It leaned more in one direction or the other. Trying to do both as is just doesnt work for me; unfortunate as i love both genres but i cant recommend it. Do try SKAs other games though its predecessor Salt and Sanctuary i cannot recommend enough, and Dishwasher vampire smile has a killer sound track.

直接上結論：
「建議喜愛一代的玩家購買前三思」
「完全把二代當成新遊戲來玩比較能夠接受」
－－－－－－－－－－－－－－－－－－－－－－－－－

製作組忘了當時一代是如何廣受玩家好評的，
就是大量「致敬」黑魂而分到魂遊戲市場的一杯羹，
而如今抄襲抄出優越感，竟然連魔物獵人都想抄，
就誕生了二代這個畫虎不成反類犬的畸形之作。


進入遊戲不久就會產生一個最大的疑問：「BOSS到底跑夠了沒」
本作為了迎合「狩獵法師」的主題，讓BOSS在地圖不斷跑動，
不只地圖怪物全部復活，還要對付新召喚的中立怪物，
大量堆怪讓追逐過程非常煩躁，浪費相當多時間在處理小怪。


製作組似乎還喜歡在地圖邊緣擺有「擊飛」技的小怪，
跑到懸崖邊常常被小怪擊落從頭再跑一次地圖，
此外小怪的「擒拿」技能太多，動畫時間又長，
逼著玩家看自己角色被怪物蹂躪又無力回天，
真不懂這樣搞玩家有何樂趣可言。


BOSS戰就能感受到製作組不夠用心在打磨遊戲，
戰鬥過程就像玩家是用50%的速度，而BOSS則是200%，
玩家喝水速度過慢、翻滾後動畫時間長，不對等的速度、
加上BOSS過快的攻擊頻率，導致沒有空檔只能單方面挨打。


相信製作組在新的一代希望能走出新的路線，
但大破大立之下卻沒設計出合理的遊戲機制，
會購買本作的很多都是看在一代的感動而入坑，
但本作卻只能說是背棄了上一代玩家的感動。


雖然大多為批評，但我在遊玩過程還是獲得不少樂趣，
洋洋灑灑寫了這麼多就是對製作組仍然抱有期待，
希望製作組不要灰心喪志，如果還有下一代能回歸初心，重拾的玩家的口碑。

盐与献祭成功展示了什么叫做野心有余能力不足。制作组知道大家很喜欢打盐与避难所的大湖女巫这场boss战，所以贴心的为大家制作了二十余个大湖女巫一样弹幕满天飞的法师供大家反复打和刷，并且为了防止大家在跑图过程中感到无聊，贴心准备了一大批野生法师乱入，每次进入地图打法师都是整个晋西北乱成一锅粥，混乱度堪比雨世界的高难度生态。可以看出本作的内容量非常庞大，每种法师都会召唤数种不同小怪，光20多种法师对应的小怪数量就非常庞大，同时还有更多武器种类对应的动作模组和战技模组，可以想到制作这些内容的工作量有多庞大。于是顺理成章的，本作在其他方面几乎全面倒退于盐与避难所，动作系统、地图设计、关卡设计、多周目数值，除去从盐与避难所祖传的问题之外剩下的全是退步，同时各方面打磨十分粗糙。可以看出，本作堆料很足，但设计方向错了堆料再多也没用，这是大家能从盐与献祭得到的教训。只能说经过制作组一年多的修改之后游玩体验尚可，在刷法师的时候野生法师乱入也能提供一些雨世界那样电子斗蛐蛐的乐子，不过远称不上优秀，至少在盐与避难所续作这个名号下完全不及格。

I have played this game for quite a few hours looking for an excuse to write a positive review. Sadly, Salt and Sacrifice is not a good game. If I wanted to compare it to Salt and Sanctuary, its predecessor, they wouldn’t even be in the same league. So I’m going to try to explain why this game is mediocre at best. It’s a metroidvania trying to be Monster Hunter, but it falls short. Boss fights are nothing more than a sad exercise in button mashing without much strategy, and almost all fights feel the same with little variation. The maps have a lot of verticality, which would make it interesting that you have to chase bosses to hunt them down, if it weren’t for the fact that absolutely any enemy (boss or mob) can send you flying with a simple attack and send you back in a rather frustrating way, if the fall doesn’t kill you. That is, if you haven’t found a trap in the middle of the map, which is there only to troll the player. One of the common mistakes that these types of games have is that they boast of having great difficulty when the reality is that their difficulty does not lie in their mechanics, but in the statistics of the enemies, which is quite frustrating. When I finished the game, I thought that a new run in NG+ mode would give me a new perspective, since the first run was too easy for me. However, I found that the enemies scale in power much more than my character can. This makes boss fights impossible in some cases where two or more bosses are in one part of the map and, in addition to the enemies that each one summons, there are also enemies from the map itself. If I can’t even see what’s happening on my screen, it’s not difficult, it’s unfair.

Salt and Sacrifice tries to be something new, but falls short and fails to shine in any of its aspects. The combat is decent, but there is nothing better than in Salt and Sanctuary. The lore, as always in these games, is cryptic enough that I didn’t even understand the story well, and it invites little exploration. The level design is decent. Overall, it’s a game that scores 5-6 out of 10. And if compared to Salt and Sanctuary, it’s a “don’t waste your money” out of 10.

Man, playing through this game was really fun.
For the story, the game has captivating lore and really surprised me a with the direction they took the narrative. 

For game-play, the game is pretty challenging in some places with a lots of stuff to do. I did my one play through and there's still loads of stuff that I probably missed. 

For character building, it's not excessively complicated but with enough variety that you can really have fun with trying out different things. I played through the game as a Sage that used Staves and Heavy armour and really just focused on 3 stats but there's plenty in the stat tree that I didn't try out. 

I'll definitely be coming back to this one.

If you are looking to get this game because you liked Salt and Sanctuary, STOP AND READ first!  This game is not like its predecessor.  I'm currently 34 hours in and will only complete this game because I spent money on it.  I don't find it fun or entertaining, just frustrating.  So lets get into it.

Compared to Salt and Sanctuary:
It does not contain in interconnected world where you can move from on zone to another.  It seems as though the developer abandoned their original ideas to follow those that are proven or expected to enhance game play.  The sanctuary         system is not in this game, preferring to use a dark souls 3 hub.  It is the only place you can level, craft, enhance equipment, and move from one zone to another.  There are NPC here, but outside of purchasing a few items they are almost entirely PVP based.  Unlike the previous game NPC do not enhance, upgrade, or transmute gear.  They literally included these NPC to talk to, dialog that is exhausted almost immediately.  There are almost no eldritch horrors bosses to fight.  The main point is to hunt down mages, that once defeated appear through out the zones mostly getting in your way.  Which wouldn't be too bad if the only real way to gain experience wasn't gotten from them and them alone, mobs drop almost no EXP!  Lastly, when compared, the interesting movement and and abilities from the previous game have been dropped in favor of those from other successful titles.  Floating up wind currents, used in Ori, fail as they are never low enough and don't actually send you up is you have any horizontal momentum.  Grappling hook, used in Halo, are basically uncontrollable and often send you flying where you are not trying to go.  The grapple points are also often place horrifically.  Magic is almost gone, this game uses weapon arts, like in Elden Ring.

As a stand alone game:
I think this game is just abysmal even when you don't compare it too Salt and Sanctuary.  I would say that it can be described as either unrefined or rushed.  Bosses are not really that unique, they all seem to fit in the exact same hit box with very generic differences, they all summon minions and their move sets are fairly easy to see.  I think they were included in the world, outside boss arena, to solely pad out the game time.  34 hours is very long for a 2d game, especially when not fun, and I still haven't beat it.  Control and movement wise, I use the same controller I use for every other game and this game just seems to have issues.  It's not the very common occurrence of the game putting attacks exactly where you are dodging to, like input reading. Its not the dodge being too far to the point where it regularly puts you out of range to attack, especially when this game seem to seek to only ever give you enough time to strike once before you need to dodge again.  The real issue is that it seems to loose button inputs.  Need to grapple to avoid falling to you death, failed to accept button input.  Trying to use a ranged weapon art requiring you to hold block then press Y, get interrupted and get locked into a stream of melee attacks that drain you stamina.  Honestly, the thing that makes this game so frustrating to me is the damage sponge enemies, the utterly excessive traps with no visible triggers, and over reliance on enemies that that hid and pin you down to stab you to death.

The developers had such a good jump off point with Salt and Sanctuary, but for whatever reason abandoned it and ended with a game that was enticing but ultimately enjoyable.

Oh god where do I start.
Firstly, I know I haven't played this game much, and I'm going to keep that in mind while writing my review. 
Like so many others in this review section, I'm an ABSOLUTE fan of Salt and Sanctuary. I've poured 300 hours of my lifetime into it, tried out every possible build (except magic because... well.. It's magic), killed every boss, nuked every boss, gotten every achievement... I just couldn't get enough.

That being said, Salt and Sacrifice lost me after I killed the fourth electromancer in a row, and those aside, I think I've killed 4 unique bosses so far and all of them were just so forgettable.. I don't even remember their names... In comparism, I'm confident that everyone here who's played Salt and Sanctuary remembers The Sodden Knight.
Back to the mages... Why do I have to play cat and mouse with them? ._. - I get you wanted to make a mage hunter, but it just feels so unrewarding running through an area you've already finished to find a boss you've already beaten, especially since some of the areas are ABSOLUTELY not fit for boss fights.
 
Next point. Random enemy spawns... No... Learning fixed patterns and spawns and capitalizing on them is, imo,  an essential part of any (FUN) souls like. And random spawns just make that impossible. I've seen a lot of people complain about repetitive enemies, but tbh I for my part haven't noticed it...
 
The combat actually feels smoother than S&S, I like the sprinting mechanic for example. It actually feels very fast paced, love it. I have one huge problem with the combat tho. No Iframes when you get knocked down is catastrophic. You can just get snowballed out of existance. I've died a couple times because an enemy launched me into the air and then homerunned me into oblivion.

Lastly, I gotta point out the obvious pros. I absolutely adore your graphic style. Please never change it. Period. 
The skill tree is as good as ever, Ive found some good weapons for my style fairly early on, and multiple for other builds had I wanted to pick smth other than pure strength :D. 
I also like the charm system, adds a layer of complexity to builds. 
The built in throwing weapon was kinda useless to me, thenagain I haven't found any strong ones yet because I haven't played the game much. 
Lastly, the map designs are mostly good, very often built either linear or circular., I just felt like players who are new to no map games might get lost at one point or another. Thenagain that's just me assuming.

Believe me when I say that I give this thumbs down with a heavy heart. I've played Dishwasher and Salt and Sanctuary and I absolutely loved both of them, and it hurts me seeing this game fail the fanbase as much as it does. I just feel Salt and Sacrifice got lost in ideas... I hope you're not discouraged to keep going by this game flopping, you both have a gift for making games, and I'd love to see  a real Salt and Sanctuary 2 someday. :3

A decent game that brings some elements from the previous Salt game, but has different gameplay loop which combines soulslike and monster hunter:
- character progress(soulslike leveling, but MH equipment crafting) 
- level structure (mostly soulslike with unlockable shortcuts and checkpoint) 
- boss fights (MH-esque where you chase a boss around the stage and end in a boss arena, but soulslike in that you have infinite retries on the fight and won't fail the hunt unless you chose to abandon) 
Which might lead to some frustration because while there is no fall damage in MH, fall damage is in full force here. 
Overall, I recommend this game so people will suffer with me, screw you Bol Gerahn

首先，这个游戏绝大部分的mod是坏的，我在nexus上找的基本就两种，要不就是bepinex不兼容，要不就是loot/monster改的data过期了。这游戏在我看来不打mod基本玩不了一点，纯纯的浪费时间，下面我来说说为什么。

这个游戏的基石就是法师狩猎，基本就是类似于怪物猎人追着个法师不断换区打。我个人认为这个模式基本和类银河城 + soullike非常不匹配，紧张的资源管理和地形杀和追猎放一起就是纯纯的坐大牢。

打猎法师也就算了，这个b法师死了还能复活，而如果你要刷这个法师你要再追一次。。。所以刷个素材还要换五六次区，不断清小怪+躲陷阱。

Dev肯定觉得自己的设计精妙无比，可能觉得地图充满了未知和随机，然而实际上地图就是乱的跟他妈坨屎一样。你进入之前的清过的地图还能遇见一群复活的法师和他们召唤的小弟，和地图原住民小怪打的有来有回的。就这样，我打开这个游戏，刷也不想刷，探索也不想探索。就是看见了就是犯恶心。

其次，这个游戏数值有大问题。我玩了个纯敏捷的角色，感觉每次碰boss都是在坐牢，boss基础攻击间隔基本都是在1-2次玩家平a之间，部分技能都是一点a不出来。我感觉是dev强行逼玩家玩远程。而且好像所有的装备都是属性底色的，所以你想推图可能要准备几套装备？？？我只能说你无敌了，要知道这个游戏是1的续作+ 类银河城，我就问问你1的物理是什么体验？类银的物理是什么体验？这个设计不能说是天马行空，真的是脑子有大病。
其实不至于这么不堪

I wanted Salt and Sanctuary II not....whatever the hell this is!

EDIT: I appreciate the dev response and the thought process behind it, but I'm telling you right now the mage hunting game is not fun at all. Like I love the look of it and the gameplay is so-so, but chasing the mages is not fun nor engaging it's just annoying. developer_response:
We're a team of two, so it was either this guy or the other guy.

The game that became Salt and Sanctuary was actually originally intended to be a mage hunting roguelike when I (James) started making some art for it in 2011 or so. I ended up on a Castlevania DS kick and decided it was safer to make something more in that vein, but after Sanctuary's success I figured maybe it was a good time to try making the mage hunting game.

review: Salt

I loved Salt and Sanctuary and this game (like many others have said) is not that game.  So while it does have some familiar stuff it takes from the first game, this should be looked at as just something else entirely.  

7/10 - Combat felt good, challenging game play, no story to speak of... Game didn't know if it wanted to be a monster hunter or souls game.  No real bugs that I encountered and the game ran very smoothly (high end pc not needed IMO).  I had fun going through the game, but I have zero desire to ever play NG+ or replay with another character.  I got it on sale and felt like the price was right for what you got, I don't think full price would have been worth it though.

This is a strong recommend from me.
10/10

Most negative reviews come from one of two places.
1. Close-minded people expecting this game to be exactly like the first. (Like how people hate Dark Souls 2 not because it was bad, but because it was different, and not what was expected)
2. People who got frustrated at one specific inconvenience/occurrence. (Like, say, getting mad that enemies were knocking them off ledges)

Salt and Sanctuary was 2D Dark Souls. Salt and Sacrifice is 2D Dark Souls AND 2D Monster Hunter. The game's formula follows Monster Hunter more closely than Dark Souls, but players of both games will feel right at home here.

I highly encourage you to look past the mixed reviews and try the game out for yourself. The game's mechanics are very streamlined and simple compared to most other games of its mixed genres. Even if you've never played Dark Souls, Monster Hunter, or any of Ska Studios' other titles, you can get into this game and have a good time. Because it's not a sequel and is barely related to Salt and Sactuary story wise as well, the game's story will not leave you with questions only answerable by another game.

Get this game.

I'm having more fun with this one than Salt and Sanctuary. While Salt and Sanctuary is very good, at this point 2D soulslike and metroidvanias became too repetitive. This game brings something new to the table, the chaotic battles in different environments make fighting the same bosses a fun experience. Also it’s much more balanced than Salt and Sanctuary, finally other than a strength build became doable. I’m 40 years old, it’s rare for a game to present me with something new, and this one is a mixture of things I saw in previous games, but it came together as something new. I wouldn’t want to play the same game again, just with a few things added to be a sequel, I’m glad this game has a new approach.
